maps.name: Ascent
maps.rotations: A–B rotate through Mid Market (fastest). CT connects both sites near spawn.
maps.notes: Mid control opens A Catwalk flank and B Market pressure simultaneously. Defending Mid Market is the #1 priority for defense.

maps.name: Bind
maps.rotations: Bind has no traditional mid — instead, two one-way teleporters connect the halves. Teleporter A exits near B Short; Teleporter B exits near A Short.
maps.notes: Teleporters enable fast rotations but make loud sounds. Timing the teleporter sound is critical intel.

maps.name: Haven
maps.rotations: C Long → C Site is fastest CT rotate. Mid Doors connect to both B and A sides. Defenders must watch 3 sites — rotations are the longest in the game.
maps.notes: Only 3-site map. Smokes for Mid Doors and Mid Top are highest priority — cutting off rotations is more important than site smokes. C Long is the longest approach; B is the fastest default.

maps.name: Icebox
maps.rotations: A–B through boiler (mid). Kitchen connects A to attacker spawn quickly.
maps.notes: Vertical map — Rafters on A and Nest on B are elevated positions that control the site. Viper Pit is the dominant ult on both sites.

maps.name: Lotus
maps.rotations: Three rotating stone doors connect mid to each site; the doors make loud sounds when used. Rotating through mid (Waterfall, Root) is key.
maps.notes: Like Haven, 3 sites stress defender rotations. The rotating doors are game-defining mechanics — timing door sounds reveals incoming rotations.

maps.name: Pearl
maps.rotations: Mid connects to both A Link and B Link. Top Mid is a critical high-ground position that enables pressure on both sites.
maps.notes: Fully enclosed underwater city map — no elevated or sky-based mechanics. Long corridors on A Main and B Main favor Op. Mid control is essential for splitting the map.

maps.name: Split
maps.rotations: A–B through Mid Top or Vent. Heaven on both sites provides elevated defender advantage.
maps.notes: Heavily defender-sided map. Heaven positions on A and B are almost mandatory to hold. A Ramps is the most contested chokepoint on attack.

maps.name: Sunset
maps.rotations: Mid Market connects A and B through the center. B Alley can be accessed from mid quickly.
maps.notes: Attacker-sided mid. A Bridge provides a high-ground look into A Site. B Lane is a long open corridor where Op is highly effective.

maps.name: Abyss
maps.rotations: Mid Bridge is a fast A–B connector. There are no map boundaries — falling off the edges is instant death.
maps.notes: No wall boundaries — certain angles and pushes near edges can result in falling off the map. Vertical peeking from ledges is a unique mechanic.
